perturbation.original_text: (vi) This covenant shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of three (3) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: (vi) This covenant shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of one (1) year thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period from three years to one year may be deemed insufficient to protect trade secrets, especially for industries where information has a longer lifespan of value.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York Uniform Trade Secrets Act (N.Y. Gen. Bus. Law § 350)
perturbation.law_explanation: While the NY Uniform Trade Secrets Act doesn't dictate a specific duration for confidentiality, it protects trade secrets as long as they remain secret and provide a competitive advantage. Reducing the confidentiality period to one year may be deemed inadequate to protect information that retains its value for a longer duration, potentially enabling misappropriation after the one-year period, thereby contradicting the Act's aim to safeguard trade secrets for their useful lifespan. Courts often consider industry standards when determining the appropriate length of confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original contract ensures confidentiality for three years after termination, whereas the changed contract reduces this period to one year. The Law Explanation states that reducing the confidentiality period might not adequately protect information that retains its value for a longer duration, potentially leading to misappropriation after the one-year period. This directly contradicts the aim of safeguarding trade secrets for their useful lifespan, as supported by the Law Explanation. Snippet 2 is unhelpful. Because the altered duration directly impacts the trade secret's protection, and the law explanation argues that a one year term might be insufficient, a legal contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be for an initial term of five (5) years, and unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement, shall automatically renew for an additional term of five (5) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be for an initial term of five (5) years, and unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement, shall automatically renew for an additional term of ten (10) years thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: Ohio law (where Global Energy is located) places restrictions on the duration of non-compete agreements. While this is not directly a non-compete agreement, a long renewal period could be seen as an unreasonable restraint on trade if it effectively prevents Global Energy from entering into similar alliances with other parties.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio Revised Code § 1331.04 addresses contracts that restrain trade. While this is a strategic alliance agreement and not a direct non-compete, extending the renewal term to ten years could be seen as an unreasonable restraint of trade if it prevents Global Energy from forming similar alliances, depending on the specific nature of the exclusivity and market conditions. This is especially true if the alliance limits Global Energy's ability to engage with competitors of Oxbow. The reasonableness of the duration would be a key factor in determining if it violates the statute.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Section 1331.04 - Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top The Legislative Service Commission staff updates the Revised Code on an ongoing basis, as it completes its act review of enacted legislation. Updates may be slower during some times of the year, depending on the volume of enacted legislation. Section 1331.04 | Conspiracy against trade prohibited. Ohio Revised Code / Title 13 Commercial Transactions / Chapter 1331 Monopolies Effective: April 6, 2017 Latest Legislation: Senate Bill 227 - 131st General Assembly PDF: Download Authenticated PDF Every combination, contract, or agreement in the form of a trust is declared to be a conspiracy against trade and illegal. No person shall engage in such conspiracy or take part therein, or aid or advise in its commission, or, as principal, manager, director, agent, servant, or employer, or in any other capacity, knowingly carry out any of the stipulations, purposes, prices, or rates, or furnish any information to assist in carrying out such purposes, or orders thereunder, or in pursuance thereof, or in any manner violate sections 1331.01 to 1331.14 of the Revised Code. Each day's violation of this section is a separate offense. Available Versions of this Section October 1, 1953 – House Bill 1 - 100th General Assembly [ View October 1, 1953 Version ] April 6, 2017 – Senate Bill 227 - 131st General Assembly [ View April 6, 2017 Version ]
perturbation.justification: The change extends the automatic renewal term from five to ten years. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation state that under Ohio Revised Code § 1331.04, such an extension could be viewed as an unreasonable restraint of trade, especially if it limits Global Energy's ability to engage with competitors. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that 'Every combination, contract, or agreement in the form of a trust is declared to be a conspiracy against trade and illegal.' The extended renewal term, if deemed an unreasonable restraint, could violate this statute.
